steps.title: Find the slope from two table points
steps.content:
The table gives $(x,y)$ pairs $(-2s, 24)$, $(-s, 21)$, $(s, 15)$. Using the first two:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{21 - 24}{-s - (-2s)} = \frac{-3}{s}$$

steps.title: Build the line and convert to standard form
steps.content:
Point-slope with $(-s, 21)$ and slope $-\tfrac{3}{s}$:
$$y - 21 = -\frac{3}{s}(x + s)$$
Multiply by $s$: $sy - 21s = -3x - 3s$, so $3x + sy = 18s$. Check with $(s, 15)$: $3(s) + s(15) = 3s + 15s = 18s$. ✓

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $sx + 3y = 18s$. Test $(-2s, 24)$: $s(-2s) + 3(24) = -2s^2 + 72$, which is not $18s$ in general. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $3x + sy = 18s$. Verified above for all three points. Matches.
  • Choice C: $3x + sy = 18$. Test $(-2s, 24)$: $3(-2s) + s(24) = 18s$, not $18$. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $sx + 3y = 18$. Test $(-2s, 24)$: $-2s^2 + 72 = 18$ forces $s^2 = 27$, not true in general. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
